title: Builder.cdiSetupDevicesInSpecdefConfig(): use configured CDI dirs
body:
#### What type of PR is this?

/kind other

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

Use the directories configured in containers.conf, now that containers/common can tell us what they are, and now that it provides a place to configure defaults for container tools, always override the library's default set, even if it's empty, which means we do nothing.

Switch to the default CDI registry instead of the recenty-deprecated non-global one.

#### How to verify it

Nominally updated integration test!

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

None

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

See also https://github.com/containers/common/pull/1834.

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *Luap99*, *nalind* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [nalind] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Tweaked so that when _no_ configuration directories are listed in configuration or passed in via API, `cdiSetupDevicesInSpec()` will skip asking the CDI package about devices, because it shouldn't be expected to know about any devices.
@nalind, you're hitting the merge conflict lottery tonight.  Other than the one outstanding question, LGTM
